vulnerability.reason: Generated describes system accuracy erosion from synthetic data misrepresentation, while reference describes human over-reliance and lack of oversight; edge-case conditions are common, but core sensitivity condition differs.
vulnerability.reference: Over-reliance on AI perception without adequate human oversight in edge-case road conditions.
vulnerability.generated: Perception system trained on synthetic rare-object samples misrepresents real-world rare concepts, eroding its accuracy in edge-case scenarios.

impact.reason: Affected party includes pedestrians but omits cyclists and adds drivers; damage type includes collisions (and more severe outcomes) but reference only mentions increased collision risk.
impact.reference: Increased collision risk for vulnerable road users (pedestrians, cyclists) during rare-object encounters.
impact.generated: Misperception of rare hazards causes vehicle control failures, resulting in collisions, injuries, and fatalities for drivers and pedestrians.

dose_response.reason: Causal arc from OOD synthetic samples to misclassification and then to collision risk in edge-case conditions is preserved, though wording differs.
dose_response.reference: Out-of-distribution synthetic rare-object samples cause perception misclassification, which in edge-case road conditions exposes drivers and pedestrians to increased collision risk.
dose_response.generated: Out-of-distribution synthetic tail-class samples from long-tail guidance diffusion models, through drivers and pedestrians relying on perception in edge-case road conditions, trigger misrepresentation of real rare concepts by the trained system, leading to misperception-caused vehicle control failures, collisions, injuries, and fatalities.
